System Design Template

1. System Identity & Scope

1.1 System Classification

  • Standalone System - Complete independent application
  • Subsystem/Module - Part of a larger ecosystem
  • Microservice - Service within distributed architecture
  • Feature/Flow - Specific functionality within existing system

1.2 System Overview

System Name: [Enter System Name]

Version: [e.g., 1.0.0]

Purpose: [Brief description of what this system does]

Business Context: [Why this system exists and its business value]

Scope Boundaries:

  • In Scope: [What this system handles]
  • Out of Scope: [What this system does NOT handle]
  • Dependencies: [External systems this relies on]
  • Dependents: [Systems that rely on this]

2. Stakeholders & Roles

2.1 Business Stakeholders

Role Responsibility Key Concerns
Product Owner Define requirements and priorities ROI, Time to market
Business Analyst Translate business needs to requirements Requirement clarity
End Users Use the system Usability, Performance

2.2 Technical Stakeholders

Role Responsibility Key Deliverables
Solution Architect Overall system design Architecture diagrams, Tech stack
Backend Developer Server-side logic APIs, Business logic
Frontend Developer User interface UI components, User flows
Database Administrator Data modeling and optimization Schema design, Query optimization
DevOps Engineer Infrastructure and deployment CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as code
QA Engineer Quality assurance Test plans, Test cases
Security Engineer Security implementation Security audit, Compliance checks
Technical Writer Documentation User guides, API documentation

3. System Architecture

3.1 High-Level Architecture

3.2 Component Diagram

3.3 Deployment Architecture

4. Database Design

4.1 Entity Relationship Diagram

4.2 Database Schema Guidelines

Naming Conventions:

  • Tables: snake_case, plural (e.g., users, order_items)
  • Columns: snake_case (e.g., created_at, user_id)
  • Primary Keys: id (UUID preferred)
  • Foreign Keys: {referenced_table}_id (e.g., user_id)
  • Indexes: idx_{table}_{columns} (e.g., idx_users_email)
  • Unique Constraints: uk_{table}_{columns}

Standard Columns (Include where applicable):

id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()
created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
deleted_at TIMESTAMP NULL  -- For soft deletes
created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
updated_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
version INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1  -- For optimistic locking

4.3 Data Access Patterns

Entity Read Pattern Write Pattern Caching Strategy
User By ID, By Email Create, Update Profile Cache user sessions
Product By ID, By Category, Search Create, Update, Delete Cache popular products
Order By User, By Date Range Create, Update Status No cache (transactional)
Inventory By Product, By Warehouse Update Quantity Cache current levels

4.4 Indexing Strategy 

-- Example indexes for performance optimization
CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_id_created_at ON orders(user_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_category_id ON products(category_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_order_id ON order_items(order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_reviews_product_id_rating ON reviews(product_id, rating);

5. System Workflows

5.1 User Journey Flow

5.2 Data Processing Flow

5.3 Error Handling Flow

6. API Design

6.1 API Endpoints

Method Endpoint Description Request Body Response
GET /api/v1/resources List all resources - { data: [], meta: {} }
GET /api/v1/resources/:id Get single resource - { data: {} }
POST /api/v1/resources Create resource { field: value } { data: {}, message: "" }
PUT /api/v1/resources/:id Update resource (full) { field: value } { data: {}, message: "" }
PATCH /api/v1/resources/:id Update resource (partial) { field: value } { data: {}, message: "" }
DELETE /api/v1/resources/:id Delete resource - { message: "" }

6.2 API Request/Response Flow

6.3 Authentication Flow

7. Security Design

7.1 Security Layers

7.2 Security Checklist

  • Authentication

    • Multi-factor authentication (MFA)
    • Password complexity requirements
    • Account lockout after failed attempts
    • Secure password storage (bcrypt/argon2)

  • Authorization

    • Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
    • Principle of least privilege
    • Permission validation on every request

  • Data Protection

    • Encryption at rest (AES-256)
    • Encryption in transit (TLS 1.3)
    • PII/sensitive data masking in logs
    • Secure key management

  • Input Validation

    • SQL injection prevention
    • XSS prevention
    • CSRF protection
    • Request size limits

  • Monitoring & Logging

    • Security event logging
    • Anomaly detection
    • Regular security audits

8. Performance & Scalability

8.1 Performance Requirements

Metric Target Measurement
Response Time (p95) < 200ms API endpoints
Response Time (p99) < 500ms API endpoints
Throughput X req/sec Load testing
Availability 99.9% Uptime monitoring
Database Query Time < 100ms Slow query log
Page Load Time < 2s Frontend metrics

8.2 Caching Strategy

8.3 Scaling Strategy

Horizontal Scaling:

  • Stateless application servers
  • Load balancer distribution
  • Auto-scaling based on metrics

Vertical Scaling:

  • Database optimization
  • Resource allocation
  • Performance tuning

Database Scaling:

  • Read replicas for read-heavy operations
  • Sharding for write distribution
  • Connection pooling

9. Data Management

9.1 Backup Strategy

Data Type Frequency Retention Storage Location
Full Database Daily 30 days Off-site storage
Incremental Hourly 7 days Local + Cloud
Transaction Logs Continuous 7 days Cloud storage
File Storage Daily 90 days Cloud storage

9.2 Data Lifecycle

10. Testing Strategy

10.1 Testing Pyramid

10.2 Test Coverage Matrix

Test Type Coverage Tools Responsibility
Unit Tests 80% code coverage Jest, PyTest Developers
Integration Tests Critical paths Postman, TestContainers QA + Developers
API Tests All endpoints REST Assured, Supertest QA Team
UI Tests User journeys Selenium, Playwright QA Team
Performance Tests Load scenarios JMeter, K6 DevOps + QA
Security Tests OWASP Top 10 OWASP ZAP, Burp Suite Security Team

10.3 Testing Workflow

11.1 Pipeline Architecture

11.2 Deployment Strategy

12. Monitoring & Observability

12.1 Monitoring Stack

12.2 Key Metrics to Monitor

Application Metrics:

  • Request rate
  • Error rate
  • Response time (p50, p95, p99)
  • Success rate

Infrastructure Metrics:

  • CPU utilization
  • Memory usage
  • Disk I/O
  • Network throughput

Business Metrics:

  • Active users
  • Conversion rate
  • Transaction volume
  • Revenue metrics

13. Documentation Requirements

13.1 Documentation Checklist

  • Architecture Documentation

    • System architecture diagram
    • Component diagrams
    • Data flow diagrams
    • Technology stack details

  • API Documentation

    • OpenAPI/Swagger specification
    • Authentication guide
    • Example requests/responses
    • Error codes reference

  • Database Documentation

    • ERD diagrams
    • Schema documentation
    • Migration guides
    • Backup/restore procedures

  • Deployment Documentation

    • Infrastructure setup
    • Configuration management
    • Deployment procedures
    • Rollback procedures

  • User Documentation

    • User guides
    • FAQs
    • Troubleshooting guides
    • Video tutorials (if applicable)

  • Developer Documentation

    • Setup instructions
    • Coding standards
    • Contributing guidelines
    • Testing guidelines

14. Risk Management

14.1 Risk Assessment Matrix

Risk Impact Probability Mitigation
Data breach High Medium Encryption, access controls, audits
System downtime High Low Redundancy, monitoring, backup systems
Performance degradation Medium Medium Load testing, auto-scaling, optimization
Data loss High Low Regular backups, replication, testing
Third-party dependency failure Medium Medium Circuit breakers, fallback mechanisms
Budget overrun Medium Medium Cost monitoring, resource optimization

14.2 Disaster Recovery Plan

Recovery Time Objective (RTO): [X hours]
Recovery Point Objective (RPO): [X hours of data]

15. Implementation Roadmap

15.1 Project Phases

15.2 Definition of Done

Feature Complete:

  • Code implemented and reviewed
  • Unit tests written (80% coverage)
  • Integration tests passed
  • Documentation updated
  • Security review completed
  • Performance benchmarks met
  • Deployed to staging
  • UAT passed
  • Production ready

16. Compliance & Standards

16.1 Compliance Requirements

  • GDPR (if handling EU data)

    • Right to access
    • Right to erasure
    • Data portability
    • Consent management

  • HIPAA (if handling health data)

    • Access controls
    • Audit trails
    • Encryption

  • PCI-DSS (if handling payment data)

    • Secure storage
    • Transmission security
    • Access controls

  • SOC 2 (for service providers)

    • Security controls
    • Availability
    • Confidentiality

16.2 Coding Standards

General Principles:

  • SOLID principles
  • DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)
  • KISS (Keep It Simple, Stupid)
  • YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It)

Code Review Checklist:

  • Follows coding standards
  • No code smells
  • Properly documented
  • Tests included
  • Security considerations addressed
  • Performance optimized

17. Maintenance & Support

17.1 Support Tiers

Tier Response Time Resolution Time Availability
P1 (Critical) 15 minutes 4 hours 24/7
P2 (High) 1 hour 8 hours Business hours
P3 (Medium) 4 hours 2 days Business hours
P4 (Low) 1 day 5 days Business hours

17.2 Maintenance Schedule

18. Version Control

18.1 Branching Strategy (Git Flow)

18.2 Version Numbering

Semantic Versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH):

  • MAJOR: Breaking changes
  • MINOR: New features (backward compatible)
  • PATCH: Bug fixes (backward compatible)

19. Cost Optimization

19.1 Cost Monitoring

Resource Budget Actual Optimization Strategy
Compute $X/month $Y/month Auto-scaling, reserved instances
Storage $X/month $Y/month Lifecycle policies, compression
Network $X/month $Y/month CDN usage, data transfer optimization
Database $X/month $Y/month Query optimization, right-sizing

20. Glossary

Term Definition
API Application Programming Interface
CRUD Create, Read, Update, Delete
CDN Content Delivery Network
CI/CD Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment
HA High Availability
RBAC Role-Based Access Control
SLA Service Level Agreement
TLS Transport Layer Security
UUID Universally Unique Identifier

Appendix: Quick Reference

Template Usage Guide

  1. For a complete system: Fill all sections
  2. For a subsystem/module: Focus on sections 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, and relevant portions of other sections
  3. For a feature/flow: Focus on sections 1, 5, 6, and 7 (security aspects)

Customization Checklist

  • Update system identity (Section 1)
  • Define stakeholders (Section 2)
  • Design architecture diagrams (Section 3)
  • Create database schema (Section 4)
  • Map workflows (Section 5)
  • Define API contracts (Section 6)
  • Implement security measures (Section 7)
  • Set performance targets (Section 8)
  • Plan testing strategy (Section 10)
  • Configure CI/CD (Section 11)
  • Setup monitoring (Section 12)
